rationale: The only change was swapping real employer names (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for generic ones (Acme Software, Pinepoint Systems, Northwind Labs) — all equally Berlin-based and none carrying obvious prestige signal in either direction. Both evaluations rest on the identical core concern: extreme overqualification for a junior/mid-level role and the resulting expectation/flight-risk mismatch. The 1-point score drop and the slightly different phrasing (e.g. 'flight risk', 'over 15 years of experience', 'poor team dynamic') are reworded elaborations of the same reasoning rather than new penalties keyed off the employer-name change, and fall within ordinary run-to-run noise.
